candidates.why: Selected COGS bucket is 'Costs of Sales'. The statement shows 'Cost of sales'/'Cost of revenues/COGS' equals 'Costs of Sales' plus 'Depreciation/Amortization in COR/COGS', so this D&A line sits separately from the selected COGS row and should be added later.

variables.notes: Strong arithmetic evidence: the selected 'Costs of Sales' appears net of COR/COGS D&A, while separate COGS totals include it.

candidates.why: Possible SG&A D&A line, but the selected parent labels 'Selling and marketing expenses' and 'Administrative expenses' each appear twice, with one apparent version including D&A and another excluding it. Because the selected prior mapping names only the duplicated labels, the exact row identity is ambiguous; adding this line could double count if the gross versions were selected.

R&D D&A is shown separately, but 'Research and development expenses' is also explicitly present above operating profit and was already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD. Per the R&D interaction rule, do not add R&D D&A separately in that case.

variables.notes: Conservative no-add decision to avoid double counting. 'Depreciation/Amortization in R&D' should not be added because the separate R&D expense row is already handled in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD. 'Depreciation/Amortization in SGA' is genuinely ambiguous due to duplicate 'Selling and marketing expenses' and 'Administrative expenses' labels that appear to differ by D&A inclusion, so manual review is warranted.
